relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus finishes the teaching and turns toward passion.
Passover and handing-over draw near.
Religious power gathers around Caiaphas.
They plan seizure by deceit.
They fear public unrest.
In Bethany, Jesus reclines in Simon’s house.
A woman pours costly ointment on his head.
The disciples call love waste.
They frame devotion as lost money for the poor.
Jesus defends the woman’s beautiful work.
The poor remain, but this moment with him is singular.
Her anointing prepares his body for burial.
Her act becomes inseparable from the good message’s memory.
Judas goes to the chief priests.
He prices betrayal in silver.
He begins seeking the moment to hand him over.
The disciples prepare the Passover question.
Jesus names his time as near.
They prepare the Passover.
Evening gathers him with the twelve.
At the table, betrayal is named.
Each disciple feels the question in grief.
The betrayer shares the bowl.
The Son of Humanity goes as written, yet betrayal carries woe.
Judas answers with the question of denial.
Bread is blessed, broken, given, and named as body.
The cup is given with thanks.
The covenant blood is poured out for release.
He points beyond the meal toward new wine in the Father’s reign.
They sing and go to the Mount of Olives.
The shepherd will be struck and the sheep scattered.
After rising, he will go before them to Galilee.
Peter insists he will not stumble.
Jesus names the coming threefold denial.
Peter and the others promise beyond their strength.
Gethsemane becomes the place of prayer.
Jesus enters grief and distress with his closest disciples nearby.
His soul is grieved to the point of death, and he asks them to watch.
He yields his will to the Father while naming the cup.
He finds them sleeping instead of watching.
Watch and pray: spirit is willing, flesh weak.
Again he yields to the Father’s will.
Heavy eyes cannot keep watch.
The third prayer repeats the surrender.
The hour arrives.
He rises toward the betrayer.
Judas comes with armed crowd.
A kiss becomes the sign of betrayal.
Rabbi is spoken with the kiss.
Jesus still calls him friend.
A sword is drawn in reactive defense.
Jesus rejects the sword-cycle.
He could call heavenly help, but does not.
The Scriptures are being fulfilled through surrender, not force.
He exposes the absurdity of secret arrest after public teaching.
The disciples flee.
Jesus is taken to Caiaphas.
Peter follows at a distance.
False witness is sought.
Many witnesses fail to establish the charge.
The temple saying becomes the accusation.
The high priest presses for answer.
Jesus is silent until asked under oath about Messiah identity.
He answers with Son of Humanity, right hand, and clouds.
The high priest names blasphemy.
The council chooses death.
Mockery and violence fall on his face.
Messiah is mocked as prophecy game.
Peter is recognized in the courtyard.
He denies knowing what is meant.
Another recognizes him at the gate.
The denial deepens with oath.
His speech gives him away.
The rooster sounds after the third denial.
Memory breaks Peter open into bitter weeping.
